audit_items.proof_trace: auctionCallback decodes the caller-supplied Call[] from data and passes it verbatim to _multiCall, which executes each Call with no restriction on target address or calldata. Because any AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE holder can construct a solution containing Call({target: tokenAddress, data: transfer(attacker, balance)}), they can drain any token held by the contract during the callback. The withdraw() function requiring DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE confirms this privilege was never intended for AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE holder calls bid() with solution=[Call({target: heldToken, data: abi.encodeWithSignature("transfer(address,uint256)", attacker, balance)})]; the auction transfers assetIn to AuctionBidder then invokes auctionCallback, which calls _multiCall executing the transfer and draining heldToken to the attacker before the auction can pull assetOut.
